programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. The chief inspector discovers that the son's future has been carefully mapped out since birth by his implacable mother Alethea, and she will not countenance any interference in her plans. However, an astonishing twist causes the detectives to reconsider the case in its entirety. programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.desc: A middle-class couple's adopted child is kidnapped, a case that takes Gently and Bacchus to a home where unmarried mothers are forced to give up their babies. But further investigations reveal a much darker side to the adoption home itself and raise questions as to how far this seemingly perfect couple were prepared to go to become parents. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Mark Gatiss, Helen Baxendale and Alison Steadman(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star(n)

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, Ryder Cup-winner Nicolas Colsaerts, Great Britain sprinter Anyika Onuora and former Celtic and Wales striker John Hartson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy asks Karen to go with him to the beauty pageant, where he makes an alarming discovery. Vinny gets involved in Keegan's business but is shocked to learn Iqra is his business partner. Whitney makes a risky decision, and Chantelle is rude to Kheerat on purpose, simply to keep Gray happy(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society tackles a legal drama, with Dennis in the lead role as unscrupulous defence lawyer Karl McKennon, taking on his ex-wife Becky as he defends an ex cop accused of murdering his brother. The designers construct a number of split sets that are wheeled in and out to create the locations described in the brutal cross examinations that take place in a courtroom. Sadly, sets are mismatched, wheeled off and on at life-threatening speeds and the courtroom itself is a quarter its proper size. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil realises Ben and Callum have broken up when his son reveals he has a date. Billy tries to reason with Honey, but when she dismisses his claims, he begins to research eating disorders, determined to help her. Vinny and Keegan are stopped in their tracks, while Whitney's good mood is short lived - and even Jack's good advice leaves her feeling more frustrated(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Comedy about a working-class couple's aspirations to achieve social acceptance and moderate material success in competitive suburbia. Gary has a building site prankster to deal with, but he turns out to be local lunatic and hardman Lee Benson. Gary sets up a one-to-one meet with Benson at his local, the roughest pub in the area, but not before he has 'rehearsed' the chat with Terri at home. Meanwhile, young Teddy King has his first ever date with 'cool girl' Rose Ferdinando. Starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: RuPaul brings the award-winning global phenomenon to the UK for the first time as 10 of the country's most fabulous drag queens compete in the 'Olympics of drag' in the hope that the host and the show's judges will crown them as the UK's first Drag Race superstar. Helping appraise the contenders are Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Andrew Garfield(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Asma Khan and Theo Randall, and a special guest faces their food heaven or food hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In this six-part series, the chef Tom Kerridge and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating lower calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for antiques at the Hemswell Antiques Centre in Lincolnshire, where experts Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help them hunt down bargains(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a champion cyclist while Ines is away - a case that inspires a little sporting rivalry between the two detectives. When the missing man's wife receives a ransom demand, evidence merges of a complex situation involving mistaken identity and murder(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the auditions, and the audience still hold the power, as only they will decide if the mirror opens for the acts or not. The dancers need to impress at least 75% of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition, while one Dance Captain will get to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows. Todrick Hall joins Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse as Dance Captains, while Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo oversee proceedings(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is still to coming to terms with Mason's death when Ryan and Dante, seemingly members of opposing street gangs, are admitted to the ED. Ruby struggles to connect with baby Harmony, and living in the shared house with Marty and Jade isn't helping. Lev again comes to the rescue and, offering support to Ruby, reassures her that she's doing fine(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Chelsea v Burnley at Stamford Bridge. Spurs had lost their last four meetings with Jurgen Klopp's Reds in all competitions, a record that Jose Mourinho will have been keen to remedy. Sean Dyche's Clarets had picked up four points from their previous two trips to west London, although Frank Lampard's men got the better of the reverse fixture in October, winning 4-2 at Turf Moor thanks to a hat-trick from American Christian Pulisic. Plus, action from Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park, Everton v Brighton & Hove Albion at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Southampton at the King Power Stadium, Manchester United v Norwich City at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and Friday night's game between Sheffield United and West Ham United at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks back at memorable Roadshow items to find out what happened next, including what was really inside a 200-year-old bottle of wine that Andy McConnell tasted in 2016. Militaria specialist Mark Smith explores the mystery of a briefcase containing items that belonged to Second World War pilot Hubert Henderson, and unearths a remarkable story that takes in mistaken identity and the Great Escape. Asian art specialist Lee Young is reunited with the owners of a rare bronze water-dropper that was valued at £10,000 at a Roadshow in Newcastle and finds out what happened when it was put up for auction earlier this year(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Exploring the stories and histories of Britain's beaches. Nicholas Crane searches for leeches in the pebble-pools of Dungeness in Kent, and learns how the area was formed. Hermione Cockburn takes to the air to view sand art in Jersey, Andy Torbet visits one of the nation's most dangerous beaches, Tessa Dunlop inspects a steelworks built on the shore of Port Talbot, Neath, and in Aberlady Bay, East Lothian, Nick Hewitt unearths submarines that played a pivotal role in sinking the Tirpitz, one of Hitler's largest battleships(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Mother and son Michelle and Jason from Southport want to spend the winter on Lanzarote - but they need a rental property matching two different tastes. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott serves up a seafood special and his catches of the day include a fish pie from Mary Berry, a crab and leek tart from the Hairy Bikers, Nigel Slater's mackerel and bacon salad, a lobster dish from James Martin and Lorraine Pascale's fish and chips(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Clare Balding is the latest celebrity to go in search of her roots, and she is particularly interested in her maternal great-grandfather. Clare believes he could have been gay, but getting to the truth of the matter proves a challenge when all the evidence comes from a time when homosexuality was illegal. Digging into her father's side of the family takes the sports presenter to New Jersey and New York where she uncovers an extraordinary dynasty and American roots stretching back generations(n)

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan hopes to get closer to wild wolves than anyone before to discover the true nature of this feared predator and heads to the north of Canada in search of a pack that has never seen people and so has no fear of humans. He begins to gain the trust of an Arctic wolf family and as time passes becomes accepted by the pack, including a female and its three newborn pups, and has some close encounters that reveal a softer side to the animals(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents a special edition, revisiting some of the historic venues from the series' history. Paul also finds out about the scandalous Georgians of Lydiard Park(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Dundee to Aberdeen, stopping off at Glamis Castle, where the Queen Mother grew up, and making an excursion into the Eastern Highlands(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the second episode, cameras follow new parents Mo and Syler as they get to grips with round-the-clock feeding and sleep deprivation, and Paul and Craig's surrogate Mel gives birth to their twins. Elsewhere, five-year-old Ezrah and seven-year-old Elijah have to come to terms with new rules in the family home after the birth of their baby sister(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: Documentary following life in a home for orphaned chimpanzees run by wildlife vet Jimmy Desmond and his wife Jenny. The Desmonds started with just two rescued baby chimps, saved from the illegal pet trade, but now their home is bursting at the seams with 21 orphans to look after. Their dream is to build them all a new sanctuary in the forest and, with help from friend and chimp specialist Ben Garrod, they teach the orphans the skills they would have learnt from their mothers in the wild(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Part two of two. The trial of Edward Cairney and Avril Jones continues, including the testimonies of Jean McSherry, the former partner of Margaret's father Derick, and Margaret's former teacher Jacqueline Cahill. The programme also follows senior investigating officer Detective Superintendent Paul Livingstone as he revisits Margaret's derelict home in Seacroft, Inverkip(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen
programme.desc: Royston Vasey faces a situation more terrible than anything it has faced before as boundary changes threaten to erase the town from the map for ever. The fight to save the community from administrative annihilation comes from unexpected and surprising directions, all of them local. Comedy, starring Reece Shearsmith, Steve Pemberton and Mark Gatiss(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen - Live Again!
programme.desc: A filmed performance of the comedy group's 2018 live show from London's Eventim Apollo, featuring Mark Gatiss, Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith as popular characters including Papa Lazarou, Edward and Tubbs, and Dr Chinnery. The show picks up where the 2017 Christmas special left off in the depths of the dreaded 'Wife Mine', and also features Mickey and Pauline sharing a bed on stage, an ambitious new production from the Legz Akimbo theatre troupe, and some audience participation with Herr Lipp(n)

programme.title: In Search of Dracula with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss explores and celebrates the icon of popular culture. He begins at Orava Castle in Slovakia, as used in the classic vampire film Nosferatu, then heads to the London Library on the trail of Bram Stoker's newly discovered research literature. In Philadelphia, he studies Stoker's hand-written notes and examines the abandoned ideas, plotlines and characters that went on to become this world-famous story. Mark also meets with actors, experts and historians as he explores the Count's transition from page to screen - and even manages to reunite seven Hammer brides. Contributors include the latest Dracula, Danish actor Claes Bang(n)

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins pioneering biologist Dr Victor Lukarevskiy in Russia to attempt to rehabilitate two pairs of lynx held captive for years. Two of these cats, Koshka and Miass, were born in the wild, but have been locked in tiny steel cages for years by a fur trapper, while their Bryansk and Dasha have never seen the wilderness, and Dr Lukarevskiy wants to give these lynx a new life in the wild. Will they have the instincts needed to survive in the wild? And after years in human captivity, will they make it?(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families promote their speciality cheeses and different forms of garlic at the Scottish Game Fair, having worked hard to get their products to market(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to cook monkfish with vermouth sauce for Marcus, while three more chefs make a crepe souffle with a caramel sauce for Monica. For the second challenge, the contestants have to prepare a dish of their own design to impress the judges and earn their place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores a diverse range of invasions as Nick Crane heads to the Channel Islands to learn about the German occupation of Guernsey during the Second World War, while Ruth Goodman goes to the Isle of Man to find out how it became the home of the TT motorcycle race, which attracts around 10,000 bikers from around the world each year(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Chris and Victoria from Cornwall enlist the help of local property expert Dolores in West Palm Beach to find a perfect rental home for them and young son Jacob in Florida. As Chris works away for weeks at a time this winter break is an opportunity for the family to spend valuable time together, but can they find a property within budget? Narrated by Gloria Hunniford(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces BBC archive recipes where the combination of ingredients initially seems a little unlikely, though the results are pleasing. The menu includes Michel Roux Junior's beef cheeks with pears, garlic and chocolate, Nigel Slater's cheesy apple pie, Rachel Khoo's figs and chicken livers and a Bake Off masterclass that combines blackcurrant and liquorice(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Adil Ray is the latest celebrity uncovering facts about his heritage in the genealogy documentary. The Citizen Kahn star's mother came to England from newly independent Kenya in 1967, and now he identifies himself as Muslim, Pakistani, African and Brummie. Adil heads to east Africa to find out more about his lineage, tracing his mixed Asian and African ancestry across Kenya to Uganda, meeting relatives for the first time in the traditional kingdom of Buganda and investigating a rumoured link to royalty(n)

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to Canada in the autumn to reunite with the Arctic wolves he has been following as they try to raise their pups and prepare for winter. The cameraman also walks with members of the pack as they hunt lemmings, musk oxen and hares and encounter a rival pack(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgin's port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Party's first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michael's journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the third episode, the families find new ways to cope as the dads go back to work. New dad Mo feels pressure to earn more cash, while Nigel is a little too glad of the break from his family of seven. Hermisha does not want Shabazz's paternity leave to end, and Paul and Craig say an emotional goodbye to Mel as they leave the hospital to begin their new life with the twins(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The keen horticulturalist visits some of the most famous and interesting gardens in the US. He begins his trek in a prairie, the original American flowering wilderness, only to find much of it has disappeared. In New York, he explores vegetables being grown on the city's rooftops, visits an enthusiastic allotment community in the Bronx and learns about the history of America's most famous public green space, Central Park. In Philadelphia, Monty examines one of the country's most famous gardens and enjoys a modern version of a prairie garden(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig indulges in a bit of 'Quaffing' and 'Scoffing', inviting Jo Brand, Phill Jupitus and Prue Leith to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Life, Death and Laughter
programme.desc: The Big Yin returns to Glasgow's famous King's Theatre, where his journey into comedy first began, to talk life, death and laughter, in a far-reaching interview with BBC Arts Editor Will Gompertz. It's a conversation that looks back on the comic's past and ahead to the future, covering all elements of his unusually packed existence - how he got started, his approach to comedy, his Scottish roots, and how Parkinson's disease is the latest thing he's having to laugh at(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen
programme.desc: The local authorities, the newspaper and the police all play their parts as the developing situation in Royston Vasey reaches an earth-shattering climax. Can the genies be put back in the bottle? And what dark forces have been unleashed in the amphibarium? Comedy, starring Mark Gatiss, Reece Shearsmith and Steve Pemberton(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary following the acclaimed comedian, actor and musician, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him move on from being a welder in Glasgow to become 'The Big Yin'. The programme features deeply personal interviews with Billy himself, alongside contributions from some of the greatest names in comedy and popular culture, who talk about Connolly's lasting influence(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: Second of a two-part documentary following the comedian, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him become 'The Big Yin'. The programme finds Billy back in the Scotland of his childhood, where he reveals that knitted woolly swimming trunks were not a figment of his imagination. Featuring contributions by friends, fans and colleagues including Eddie Izzard, Ross Noble, Micky Flanagan, AL Kennedy, Tracey Ullman, Val McDermid, Sharleen Spiteri and Eddi Reader(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Spectacular moments from the series as cameras capture the extraordinary variety of life found in South America and visit Asia, the largest of all continents, that hides rarely seen creatures. The sometimes surprising cities of Europe are explored, as are the wilds of Africa, home to the greatest gatherings of animals. The searing heat of Australia boasts weird and wonderful wildlife, while North America is home to pioneering animals that make the most of every opportunity. Finally, there is a trip to the frozen wilderness of Antarctica(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson play video clips featuring a mysterious web that covered an entire street, and an Australian sea turned into a foam bath(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Lauren Layfield, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall returns to Africa in his search for the world's deadliest creatures. He encounters the spotted hyena, capable of tearing prey apart with its super-strong jaws, the gelada baboon, with a pair of fangs that would make a vampire jealous, and an animal known locally as the black death - the Cape buffalo. He also comes across the snake-munching ground hornbill and the regal crowned eagle, capable of snatching unsuspecting monkeys from the treetops(n)

programme.title: Supersized Earth
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell moves on to transport as he continues his investigation into engineering innovations over recent decades. He takes a treacherous walk along what will be one of the longest suspension bridges in the world, rides in a robot car and sees how far aeroplanes have advanced by flying a replica of the Wright brothers' glider from 1902. Along the way he discovers how to transport an object the size of Buckingham Palace halfway around the world and the advances in technology that have now made it possible to build a permanent house in space(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles between homemade fighting robots, including a towering, sword-wielding warrior built by a Mensa member and his three sons, that is one of the tallest machines to ever enter the arena and runs on wheels that are the height of a washing machine. Other contestants include a mechanical lobster and a 19-year-old self-taught engineer's creation, which has teeth that spin at 9000RPM. The conflict takes a dramatic turn when one of the robots bursts into flames(n)

programme.title: The Odd Life of Timothy Green (2012)
programme.desc: A couple bury a box containing their wishes for the perfect child they are unable to have. Soon afterwards, a mysterious boy with leaves growing from his feet shows up on their doorstep, claiming to be their son. The strange youngster soon transforms the lives of not only his parents, but their whole community. Fantasy, starring Jennifer Garner and Joel Edgerton(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Watts Gallery in Surrey, where David Harper and Thomas Plant find unusual film and TV memorabilia and some stunning French cameo glass(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at the career of the Oscar-winning actor through interviews conducted with the BBC. Hopkins talks about his approach to acting, his years as a hellraiser, and the films that helped make him a star, including Remains of the Day, Shadowlands, Nixon, and The Silence of the Lambs, in which he first portrayed his most celebrated character, Dr Hannibal Lecter. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small (1975)
programme.desc: A new apprentice to an eccentric vet in the Yorkshire Dales quickly discovers life in the country is not as idyllic as he thought. However, as he finds his feet, the newcomer begins to romance the daughter of a local farmer. Drama based on the series of books by James Herriot, starring Simon Ward, Anthony Hopkins, Lisa Harrow, Freddie Jones and TP McKenna(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The keen horticulturalist visits some of the most famous and interesting gardens in the US. He begins his trek in a prairie, the original American flowering wilderness, only to find much of it has disappeared. In New York, he explores vegetables being grown on the city's rooftops, visits an enthusiastic allotment community in the Bronx and learns about the history of America's most famous public green space, Central Park. In Philadelphia, Monty examines one of the country's most famous gardens and enjoys a modern version of a prairie garden(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin travel to Switzerland in search of four extraordinary homes. They begin their journey in a tiny hamlet called Jeurs and visit a four-bedroom house, before travelling to Brissago to see a property with views across Lake Maggiore. Next, the pair head to a remarkable 'S'-shaped home outside of Zurich, before reaching the final house overlooking Lake Lucerne(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan continues his journey around the equator, witnessing the fight to preserve the Amazon rainforest from destruction. He joins an aerial police patrol hunting for illegal loggers and witnesses a tribe mapping their territory to prevent it from being destroyed. Crossing the Andes, Gordon discovers how aerial surveillance is used to monitor Ecuador's endangered condors, and discovers a nature reserve in the canopy of a pristine patch of cloud forest, where new species are being discovered(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The world's biggest beasts have always captured people's imagination. But while being big can have its advantages, it also comes with sizeable challenges. From the Komodo dragon, whose huge appetite means it must take on prey 10 times its weight, to the giraffe, whose long neck must control immense blood pressure, nature's biggest beasts must go to extraordinary lengths to thrive. This film tells just some of these survival stories. Narrated by Miles Jupp(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring seizes the chance to make a real contribution to the war effort when his platoon is ordered to guard a captured German U-boat crew until they can be transferred to a PoW camp. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, guest starring Philip Madoc, with Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and Ian Lavender(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans(n)

programme.title: Joanna & Jennifer: Absolutely Champers
programme.desc: In Absolutely Fabulous, Patsy and Edina were rarely seen without a glass of champagne in their hands. And it turns out their real-life counterparts, comedy collaborators and great friends Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, also like nothing more than getting together to share a glass of something chilled and bubbly. Now the pair head across the channel to explore the Champagne region of France and find out how the tipple is made. Along the way, they also quiz each other about their lives, revealing previously unheard stories and anecdotes(n)

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha(n)

programme.title: Sara Pascoe Live: LadsLadsLads
programme.desc: The comedian performs the finale of her critically acclaimed 2017 show at the London Palladium, charting her efforts to be happily single after the break-up of a four-year relationship and oversharing about her imaginary brother Stephen(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: An immersive journey into the world of a green sea turtle, using only natural sounds and embedded graphics to deliver detailed information about its daily routine(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons, beginning on New Year's Day, with the landscape in the grip of winter. Time-lapse photography shows a magical country shrouded in frost and swirling mist, while water becomes the enemy as the creatures must cope with ice. Red squirrels resort to subterfuge, kites track a farmer's plough to get at the worms beneath the frost, and as winter fades adders bask in the sun and the woodland floor erupts with snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Gravity and Me: The Force That Shapes Our Lives
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili investigates the amazing science of gravity, recreating groundbreaking experiments, including the moment when Galileo first worked out how to measure it. He investigates gravitational waves, finds out from astronauts what it's like to live without gravity, sets out to find where in Britain gravity is weakest and so where we weigh the least, and helps design a smartphone app that volunteers use to demonstrate how gravity affects time and makes us age at slightly different rates(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Examining Dark Energy, the mysterious force that is unexpectedly causing the universe's expansion to speed up. Its effects were discovered in 1998, but physicists still do not know what it is, and its very existence calls into question Albert Einstein's General Theory of Relativity; the cornerstone of modern physics. The hunt for the identity of Dark Energy is on, and although experiments conducted on earth and in space generate data that might provide a clue, physics is hoping another Einstein might emerge and write a new theory that explains the mystery(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Winning
programme.desc: A witty, mind-expanding guide in which Hannah Fry unlocks the essential strategies a person needs to get what they want - to win more of the time. The mathematician also hails 20th-century scientists John von Neumann and John Nash, who worked out the science of success and reveals how the same rules apply to the world's biggest problems - whether its avoiding nuclear annihilation or tackling climate change(n)

programme.title: Akala's Odyssey
programme.desc: The rapper and poet explores the world of Homer and the Odyssey, to discover how an epic poem became the cornerstone of Western literature and how his own experiences have been impacted by a 3,000-year-old classic. Akala travels on a personal journey from the ruins of Troy to the high city of the Acropolis, looking at how language can create national identity and how the Odyssey endures today(n)

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Art historian Helen Rosslyn opens the doors of impressive country houses to tell the stories of how so many great paintings came to Britain, and of the people who brought them here. In the first episode, she reveals the influence of the 17th-century pioneer collectors, including Thomas Howard (the Earl of Arundel) and King Charles I and his entourage known as the Whitehall Group, and explores how the latter introduced a taste for the Baroque to the country, commissioning artists such as Rubens, Van Dyke and Antonio Verrio(n)

programme.title: Gravity and Me: The Force That Shapes Our Lives
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili investigates the amazing science of gravity, recreating groundbreaking experiments, including the moment when Galileo first worked out how to measure it. He investigates gravitational waves, finds out from astronauts what it's like to live without gravity, sets out to find where in Britain gravity is weakest and so where we weigh the least, and helps design a smartphone app that volunteers use to demonstrate how gravity affects time and makes us age at slightly different rates(n)

programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on January 5, featuring performances by Erasure, Kim Wilde, a-ha, Duran Duran, Boy Meets Girl, Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Climie Fisher, Inner City, Neneh Cherry, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan and the Four Tops(n)

programme.title: Easy Listening Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by some of the best-known easy-listening musicians of the 1960s and 70s, including Sergio Mendes & Brasil 66, Burt Bacharach, the Carpenters and Andy Williams(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Sybil Ruscoe and Simon Mayo present the edition first shown on January 12, featuring performances by the Darling Buds, Will to Power, Milli Vanilli, Duran Duran, Boy Meets Girl, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock, Mike and the Mechanics, Roachford, Fine Young Cannibals, Roy Orbison, Freiheit, Cookie Crew, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, and Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: Forever Punk
programme.desc: The naturalist, environmentalist and life-long punk reveals how, as a teenager with undiagnosed Asperger's, punk rock may have saved his life. By giving him a purpose, he was able to harness his creativity, which led to him becoming a TV presenter with a determination to champion wildlife. In a highly personal and revelatory film, Chris sets out to question both himself and other former punks who, like him, rocked against racism, fought for gay rights and caused their parents untold grief, to discover if the values they all believed in still hold true today(n)

programme.title: Punk Britannia at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of BBC studio sessions from 1975 to 1981 by the British bands that broke into the charts courtesy of punk, featuring archive performances by groups including Dr Feelgood, Eddie and the Hot Rods, Gang of Four, the Sex Pistols, the Clash, Joy Division, the Damned, Siouxsie and the Banshees, the Damned and Buzzcocks(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm(n)

programme.title: I Can Go for That: The Smooth World of Yacht Rock
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Katie Puckrik revisits a halcyon period of Los Angeles studio craft when studio-based artists such as the Doobie Brothers, Steely Dan and Hall & Oates produced the smoothest R'n'B and married it to adult themes about longing, aspiration and melancholy. In its day this music was never identified as a genre, but in the 21st Century, in a nod to its finely crafted nature, it's come to be known as yacht rock. Katie looks at the singer-songwriters of Laurel Canyon as well as soft rock pioneers such as the band America. Contributors include Jay Graydon, John Oates, Mark Ronson and JD Ryznar(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: Forever Punk
programme.desc: The naturalist, environmentalist and life-long punk reveals how, as a teenager with undiagnosed Asperger's, punk rock may have saved his life. By giving him a purpose, he was able to harness his creativity, which led to him becoming a TV presenter with a determination to champion wildlife. programme.title: Icelandic Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a 38-mile journey along Iceland's most famous hiking route. She navigates daunting mountain climbs, red-hot lava fields, freezing river crossings and deadly clouds of sulphuric gas to reach the volcanic crater at the centre of the Eyjafjallajokull glacier(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present the ultimate guide to the Cornish coast, from the River Tamar to Tintagel Castle, as they discover what makes the stretch so unique. As well as selecting some of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years, Tessa takes a ride on a variety of boats to learn the untold secrets of the Cornish shores. The presenters go line fishing with a local Looe fisherman, explore serpentine rock on The Lizard with a leading geologist, uncover a story of tragedy at sea, and find out what it is like to live in the storm-hit harbour of Porthleven(n)

programme.desc: Someone stops the samba at the Rio Carnival. Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Sinker sends Cook and Line a message in a bottle. Ryan, Reece, Charlie-May and Isabelle play Coconutty Raft and Tumbly Barrel before taking on the Shipwreck Rummage to try and win back Gem's jewels(n)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day heads to the rainforests of Borneo in search of a treeshrew and its favourite food. Along the way, he encounters a tree snake and some hungry wild pigs(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gramps teaches Waffle to read while Mrs Hobbs is dog-sitting Jelly next door(n)

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Tim arrives at Tony Welch's house for a playdate, where he helps Mr Welch with a DIY job in the garden and bonds with Tony's best friend Sam(n)

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: After playing with DIY Derek's dog at home, Topsy and Mum go to Grandma's house, where Topsy examines her dog Mossy using her toy vet set and cheers her up with a tennis ball(n)

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Feijoada is a Brazilian stew containing black beans and many other delicious ingredients(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Struay must win their upcoming shinty match against Coll to keep the cup, so Neilly Beag trains extra hard to make sure he is picked for the team(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Duck has been leaving his toys everywhere, so Sarah decides to help him tidy his bedroom(n)

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley helps to harvest mistletoe, JB Gill visits a tractor factory and Rory Crawford finds out about hedgehogs. Storm also shares a snowy poem and reveals how soup is made(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we meet three children enjoying a very busy day.In Italy, seven-year-old Mario makes a birthday card for his dad - with a beautiful poem. In India, six-year-old Anushka goes on a journey to meet her family in the countryside. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's seven, makes a kite with his family - and flies it from their roof. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ottalie goes on a trip to visit her Grandma, Grandpa and Heidi the pony(n)

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo always has something on his mind, so Lily and Felix help him find out how his thought processes work(n)

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits Marlee, who is taking her pet dog Missy to the vet. Rory learns how the vet at a zoo keeps flies from bothering some very special rhinos(n)

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Woolly steps in to help when Tig loses her hat, scarf and gloves on the way home from the botanic glass house(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody fly a kite, and later the youngster asks for some music about hills or mountains(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble and Grandad Tumble go up and down on a see-saw, while Justin and his friends have fun at the seaside and go up and down on a cliff lift(n)

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: In New Zealand, baby fur seals choose a safe pool in a river. In Antarctica, Adelie penguin chicks have to learn how to sink. Polar bears live on the ice, but they're great in the Arctic water too. Presented by Andy Day(n)

programme.title: Junk Rescue
programme.desc: Danny finds a bundle of woolly scarfs and jumpers with holes in them and takes some of them to Ashleigh the Weaver. Zoe and the Junkyard Helpers make new things from the rest(n)

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: The duo are excited about their hat-tastic fashion show - until all the hats disappear(n)

programme.title: Puss In Boots
programme.desc: Join Maddie, Danny, the Junkyard Helpers and Northern Ballet for CBeebies Puss in Boots(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Professor Millie's Jam Tart Machine causes a sticky situation(n)

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children(n)

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari asks her friend Barry how he came to be so good at windsurfing, and discovers that the secret to developing any skill is to keep on trying - even when you fail(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African folk tale animation telling the story of why wildebeest stampede(n)

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: The Lost Pirate's search for buried treasure makes a mess in the GiggleQuiz studio(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Bella and the other children become transfixed on a beautiful rainbow that forms over the estate(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Liam and Katie Morag choose special presents for Grannie Island on Grandmother's Day, but their plan to deliver them to her are threatened(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah, Duck and Bug set off on a big adventure, through uncharted territory, as they attempt to cycle all the way around the park(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola finds a toy rabbit near school and is worried about it. Charlie puts it on the school wall, but when it is still there later in the day, Lola insists they take it home(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy teaches Igglepiggle how to kick a ball around the garden(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Isla Fisher reads Big!, Tim Hopgood's tale about a small boy who longs to be bigger(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville tell the story of travellers turned away from cruises because they were expecting a baby and the case of one unhappy couple who returned from their Caribbean wedding still legally unhitched. Plus, the bereaved families who found little sympathy from their travel companies(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman profiles fireman Matt Parsons, who wants to move to Australia, but wife Jessica is not so keen. It remains to be seen whether a week in Melbourne will change her mind(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shopkeeper grapples a gun from the hands of an armed robber, a retiree is duped on his own doorstep by a master scammer and a fraudster tries to claim compensation after a fall(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Edinburgh, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visiting the city's famous underground vaults(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie takes samples of her DNA for testing, Joe has an eye infection and Karen is worried about Jimmi. Ayesha uncovers a secret while helping a school out with injections(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits the Isle of Wight with a couple who originally had ambitions to carve out a new life for them and their son while tapping into the island's tourist trade(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families promote their speciality cheeses and different forms of garlic at the Scottish Game Fair, having worked hard to get their products to market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest including 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Leo starts work on the market, Martin and Kush warn him to stay out of everyone's way. Gray quizzes Chantelle about Jags again, prompting her to keep her distance from the Panesars. Karen is angry with Honey when she hears Janet is not taking part in the pageant(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The return of the whodunit set on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as the locals set out to celebrate the New Year - only for the festivities to be cut short when a masked man stabs a woman in her own home, providing Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team with yet another murder to solve. Ardal O'Hanlon stars as the Irish detective - although as has been widely reported, he will soon be leaving the lead role - with guest appearances by Adrian Edmondson, Nina Wadia and Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Oxford, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The case of the passengers promised 750 euros to get off an over-booked flight, only to have the amount reduced once they'd stepped off the plane. Plus, why the costs of taking a pet abroad is so much higher in Britain than elsewhere and the disabled travellers forced to drive across a continent because an airline refused to accommodate them. Presented by Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits Needham Market near Ipswich to appraise a cottage, before heading to a development in Newport, Wales. Dion Dublin looks round a two-bed home in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Property series in which people get the chance to experience life in Australia or New Zealand before deciding whether they want to make the move permanently(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: The tenacious detective who trailed the criminal who held a taxi driver at knifepoint and carjacked an elderly woman. Plus, the actions of a man horrified when a thief stole a charity tin of the hospice where his mum spent her last days. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston. Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings and also attempts a spot of Morris Dancing, discovering the history of the humble clog and the part it played in Lancashire's industrial heritage(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi has to deal with a riot at the prison, while Zara tries to catch Daniel out. Al is thrown when a patient wants him to fix a broken laptop(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After a mysterious thief keeps stealing valuable treasures from under his nose, Hercule Flambeau visits Kembleford to see if Father Brown can help him to eliminate the competition. Mark Williams and John Light star in the crime drama(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson travels to the counties of Somerset and Shropshire as he catches up with past house hunters who have opened doors to a better quality of life in the countryside. First, Jules calls in on a couple of rolling stones who are now living the country dream in Somerset. He then heads Shropshire Hills to meet a retired policeman who wanted to get as far away from the hustle and bustle of city life as possible(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Catherine Southon hit the final leg of their challenge, travelling through Cambridgeshire and Suffolk before heading to auction at Lincolnshire. Which one of them will make the biggest profit?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard is challenged to cook a calorific banquet for the Dragon Pro Wrestlers in Newport. These professional athletes pack a whopping punch when it comes to their diet eating up to 4,000 calories when preparing for a bout. It remains to be seen therefore whether Matt's vegan meal can provide them with the energy needed to get them though to an important, upcoming wrestling event(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy asks Karen to go with him to the beauty pageant, where he makes an alarming discovery. Vinny gets involved in Keegan's business but is shocked to learn Iqra is his business partner. Whitney makes a risky decision, and Chantelle is rude to Kheerat on purpose, simply to keep Gray happy(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society tackles a legal drama, with Dennis in the lead role as unscrupulous defence lawyer Karl McKennon, taking on his ex-wife Becky as he defends an ex cop accused of murdering his brother. The designers construct a number of split sets that are wheeled in and out to create the locations described in the brutal cross examinations that take place in a courtroom. Sadly, sets are mismatched, wheeled off and on at life-threatening speeds and the courtroom itself is a quarter its proper size. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil realises Ben and Callum have broken up when his son reveals he has a date. Billy tries to reason with Honey, but when she dismisses his claims, he begins to research eating disorders, determined to help her. Vinny and Keegan are stopped in their tracks, while Whitney's good mood is short lived - and even Jack's good advice leaves her feeling more frustrated(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Comedy about a family of chancers, low on cash but high on spirit - determined to survive through any means necessary. Its members run a faltering catering business and a few other dubious things on the side. In this opening episode, someone has swiped Glyn's wing mirrors and he is out for revenge. Meanwhile, family head Peggy is having the time of her life - but with who? Written by and starring Steve Speirs, with Robert Pugh, Alexandria Riley, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Jams Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Comedy about a working-class couple's aspirations to achieve social acceptance and moderate material success in competitive suburbia. Gary has a building site prankster to deal with, but he turns out to be local lunatic and hardman Lee Benson. Gary sets up a one-to-one meet with Benson at his local, the roughest pub in the area, but not before he has 'rehearsed' the chat with Terri at home. Meanwhile, young Teddy King has his first ever date with 'cool girl' Rose Ferdinando. Starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: RuPaul brings the award-winning global phenomenon to the UK for the first time as 10 of the country's most fabulous drag queens compete in the 'Olympics of drag' in the hope that the host and the show's judges will crown them as the UK's first Drag Race superstar. Helping appraise the contenders are Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Andrew Garfield(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Asma Khan and Theo Randall, and a special guest faces their food heaven or food hell(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard is challenged to cook a calorific banquet for the Dragon Pro Wrestlers in Newport. These professional athletes pack a whopping punch when it comes to their diet eating up to 4,000 calories when preparing for a bout. It remains to be seen therefore whether Matt's vegan meal can provide them with the energy needed to get them though to an important, upcoming wrestling event(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for antiques at the Hemswell Antiques Centre in Lincolnshire, where experts Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help them hunt down bargains(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Devizes, Wiltshire, designing a wildlife-friendly garden that must also be ideal for everyday family life - on a budget of £1,500, with plans including upcycling old pallets(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, Ryder Cup-winner Nicolas Colsaerts, Great Britain sprinter Anyika Onuora and former Celtic and Wales striker John Hartson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a champion cyclist while Ines is away - a case that inspires a little sporting rivalry between the two detectives. When the missing man's wife receives a ransom demand, evidence merges of a complex situation involving mistaken identity and murder(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the auditions, and the audience still hold the power, as only they will decide if the mirror opens for the acts or not. The dancers need to impress at least 75% of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition, while one Dance Captain will get to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows. Todrick Hall joins Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse as Dance Captains, while Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo oversee proceedings(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is still to coming to terms with Mason's death when Ryan and Dante, seemingly members of opposing street gangs, are admitted to the ED. Ruby struggles to connect with baby Harmony, and living in the shared house with Marty and Jade isn't helping. Lev again comes to the rescue and, offering support to Ruby, reassures her that she's doing fine(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Chelsea v Burnley at Stamford Bridge. Spurs had lost their last four meetings with Jurgen Klopp's Reds in all competitions, a record that Jose Mourinho will have been keen to remedy. Sean Dyche's Clarets had picked up four points from their previous two trips to west London, although Frank Lampard's men got the better of the reverse fixture in October, winning 4-2 at Turf Moor thanks to a hat-trick from American Christian Pulisic. Plus, action from Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park, Everton v Brighton & Hove Albion at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Southampton at the King Power Stadium, Manchester United v Norwich City at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and Friday night's game between Sheffield United and West Ham United at Bramall Lane(n)
