Meanwhile, Howard finds Stuart's relationship with his mother strange, and Penny has an interview for a sales job at Bernadette's workplace. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to teach a course at the university, and Howard surprises the gang when he decides to enrol on it. Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs

Meanwhile, Beverly dons a costume so she can attend a teenager's Halloween party in secret and protect Barry from social humiliation. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry is thrilled by the freedom his new driving licence brings, until Beverly insists he sticks to her 10 rules of the road at all times. Meanwhile, Pops refuses to wear an emergency alert bracelet and is forced to call Adam when he suffers a fall. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Pimento and Diaz decide to tie the knot, the squad members try to prepare the perfect wedding under Santiago's careful direction. Peralta and Gina help Pimento to find his grandmother's ruby earrings so that Diaz can wear them at the ceremony. Jason Mantzoukas guest stars(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is caught in a battle of wills between Sheldon and Penny, while Leonard tries to reignite his long-distance relationship with Priya, who has moved back to India. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Jim Parsons(n)

programme.desc: Leonard inadvertently gives the impression he is attracted to Amy when he accompanies her to a colleague's wedding. Meanwhile, Howard tries to persuade Bernadette they should live with his mother after getting married and suggests a trial run of the arrangement over the weekend(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Fate presents Molly with an opportunity after she finds one of her former fourth-grade students homeless and pregnant(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The young genius is forced to tutor Georgie when poor grades threaten his brother's future on the football team. In doing so, he manages to pick up a few skills himself. US comedy, starring Iain Armitage and Montana Jordan(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The young genius moves to Dallas to attend a school for gifted children, the family struggles to cope without him around any more. US comedy, starring Iain Armitage(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs

Comedy, starring Jeff Garlin and Wendi McLendon-Covey(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray struggles to get Adam interested in hockey, but he doesn't enjoy the sport and fakes enthusiasm to please his father. Beverly tries to help Erica prepare for her college entrance exams, but her plans threaten to backfire when she does the job too well(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords wants to file an official complaint against a fellow police officer who stops him in his own neighbourhood while he is off duty, but Captain Holt has other ideas. Peralta and Santiago glimpse the difficulties of parenthood when they babysit for Jeffords' children(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The officers head off for the annual conference, which is usually a good excuse for a big party. But their fun is ruined when Holt asks them to be on their best behaviour(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny catches the eye of comic-book shopkeeper Stuart, and Leonard is devastated when she gives serious consideration to dating her new admirer. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Detective Jake Peralta and his eclectic group of colleagues at Brooklyn's 99th Precinct are used to having a commanding officer who doesn't make them work too hard or follow the rules too closely. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is assigned to a minor graffiti case by Captain Holt as punishment for turning up late for work, but the investigation becomes a major headache when the vandal's identity is revealed. Meanwhile, Gina's psychic friend Carlene visits the precinct to help with a case, and upsets Charles with a prediction about his romantic life. American comedy, starring Andy Samberg(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For the first time in his career, Jake has a growing number of cases he just can't seem to solve, and the other detectives worry his run of bad luck will rub off on them. Holt asks Amy to take charge of a community outreach programme for at-risk youths, and Boyle helps Jeffords with a tough personal assignment. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake meets an attractive medical examiner at a crime scene, but annoys his colleagues when his attempts to get together with her cause a hold-up with an autopsy report. Meanwhile, Amy discovers Jeffords has some hidden talents when the police sketch artist goes off sick(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle look into an old case belonging to Hitchcock and Scully from the 1980s. The uniformed officers and detectives fight over resources at the precinct building(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Gina attend their 20th anniversary high-school reunion, while at the precinct, Charles helps Rosa with her love life and the others get caught up in a radio competition(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Gina spends time with each member of the squad, playing a high-stakes game of chess with Captain Holt, going out for a fancy lunch with Diaz and Santiago, infiltrating an expensive social club with Peralta and finding special gifts for the team(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon helps Leonard drop off a parcel for Penny, but when he sees how messy her room is, he tries to do a good deed - which is not well received. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is upset to learn Penny is dating someone, so his friends encourage him to seek a new romance by asking another woman out. Sara Gilbert appears alongside her Roseanne co-star Johnny Galecki(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard isn't thrilled when he finds out Penny is considering taking a new job that has been offered to her by her former boyfriend Zack. Sheldon attempts to develop his brain further by learning new skills(n)

However, he is deflected from his mercenary quest to find the hoard by an encounter with a tenacious doctor, whose battle to stop the spread of a deadly disease could be linked to his own mission. Adventure, starring Matthew McConaughey, Penelope Cruz and Steve Zahn(n)

Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel and Katee Sackhoff(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Shows including All Star Musicals, Mary Berry's Quick Cooking, This Morning, Harry's Heroes: The Full English, The Great Celebrity Bake Off for SU2C, The Yorkshire Vet, and The Bay are subjected to the householders' instant reactions. Narrated by Craig Cash. First aired in March 2019(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Nick and Natalie Hammond are selling their family home in Hertfordshire and want to buy somewhere near the coast with more outdoor space for their four children. Nick's keen on a move to Majorca, having holidayed in Spain as a child, but Natalie would prefer to move to Cornwall. They have a cash budget of £275,000 if they buy in Majorca where Laura Hamilton is on hand to help, or £375,000 if they buy in Cornwall, where Jonnie Irwin tries to beat Laura's properties(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Teenage sweethearts Laura and Stuart Queen have lived in the same house in Carlisle for 16 years but now want a change of location and lifestyle. Stuart wants to move to central Scotland, close to family and work. Horse-lover Laura thinks they'll get more property and land in Murcia in Spain. Jonnie Irwin tries to please them both with a budget of £150,000 in central Scotland and Laura Hamilton tempts them with properties in Spain for a budget of £120,000(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host Dairy Week, with Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood judging the baker's efforts. The first of the three challenges is a decorative signature bake that involves a tricky turn-out. The bakers then move onto the technical challenge, which is a complex recipe with a long history that dates back to Henry VIII. With the first day behind them, the contestants regroup for the final challenge of the week - an intricate showstopper usually found at the centre of Indian weddings(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The Hi Way elephants have been hit with a potentially deadly virus, with the youngest members of the family most at risk, and the keepers and Chester Zoo's veterinary team do all they can to combat the mysterious illness. Meanwhile, Mac the stallion zebra is introduced to the zoo's three mares in the hope they will produce much needed offspring for this endangered species. There's also a look into the bizarre world of the naked mole rat(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Amber and Martin Brammah in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, who are undecided on whether they should sell their home or completely renovate it. Amber owns a fitness company and runs classes from home, and feels a bigger property would be better for her business. However, Martin feels that it would be better to use their budget to renovate their current family home(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Countdown

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. And as if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max bonds with the patients undergoing chemotherapy with him but a conversation leads him to struggle with the reality of his situation. Reynolds has difficulty handling a personal matter regarding a patient's family and Frome battles with a revelation about his daughter(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Across the UK, around 2,200 women go into labour every day. Most make it to hospital, but for those who cannot, the ambulance service is there to help. The programme features call handlers giving worried partners advice while staff race to deliver the baby, and reveals how some new parents overreact and call 999 at the first sign of a cough or a bump to the head(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz with guest captain Claudia Winkleman and Johnny Vegas going up against Jon Richardson and Reginald D Hunter. Sara Pascoe joins resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the conclusion of Gordon Ramsay's visit to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, he confronts sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum, and finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the Italian restaurant's failings(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max bonds with the patients undergoing chemotherapy with him but a conversation leads him to struggle with the reality of his situation. Reynolds has difficulty handling a personal matter regarding a patient's family and Frome battles with a revelation about his daughter(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Thailand to find out if the alarming headlines about additive MSG are justified, Kate Quilton heads deep into the Amazon rainforest to investigate rumours that the Brazil nuts on UK supermarket shelves are radioactive and Matt Tebutt reports on a Lincolnshire business producing 1.5 million bottles of elderflower cordial without employing a single flower picker(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Chris and Sherrine Kingsley-Smith have spent most of their 14-year marriage travelling the world on a boat but are now ready to drop anchor for dry land. Chris is keen on the rural lifestyle of the Cotswolds, where they have friends, but Sherrine has her heart set on the Dordogne region of France, where they're currently renting. Jonnie Irwin shows them places in the UK for their budget of £700,000, while Jasmine Harman tempts them with some amazing properties in France for up to £900,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Syd and Caroline Keenan from Cheshire want to buy a holiday home for their family but can't decide whether it should be in Wales's Llyn Peninsula, where Caroline has strong family connections, or Sesimbra in Portugal: a place Syd loves for its culture, cuisine and climate. Jonnie Irwin shows them fantastic properties in Wales, while Jasmine Harman has great homes in Portugal for Syd and Caroline's budget of £250,000(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig introduce a special round of the baking challenge in which contestants must create confectionery inspired by the Roaring Twenties. The signature challenge involves a decorative twist on custard pies, while the technical round will be a test of the bakers' timing and consistency. Finally, for the Shows, they must create a three-tiered masterpiece inspired by their tipple of choice. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith are on hand to assess the results of their efforts and decide who will be leaving the tent(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition gets under way at the Oak Tree Cottage in Burley, Hampshire, where owner Jacqui Castle and her sidekick Jane Waters hope the homely approach will win the day. Guests Martin and Catherine Crosswaite are not encouraged by the shared facilities, while breakfast served at a communal table is not to the liking of Norman Butcher and Lorraine Watkins(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Bath's the Rising Sun Inn, where owners Lorraine Hawkins and Norman Butcher hope to bring their guests up to standard by having a dress code. Following their feedback on the last visit, Jacqui and Jane take an extra critical eye to the rooms, while Martin and Catherine show their competitive streak during a boules match(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Busfeild Arms in East Morton for the third day of the contest, where hosts Martin and Catherine Croswaite pride themselves in giving great Yorkshire hospitality. Jacqui is overwhelmed by the luxury and extras in the rooms, which raises questions about how the owners make a profit. Meanwhile, Martin shocks the group by sharing his stunt motorbike riding past, and later, Patrick feels the pressure of being the final host(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stopover is at Patrick Duggan's Beatles-themed Epstein House in Liverpool, the former home of band manager Brian Epstein's father. Jacqui and Jane are unimpressed by unpleasant smells in the bedroom, while Martin and Catherine show they will do anything to win during a game of Fab Four charades. At dinner, Patrick shocks his guests when he reveals his idea of the ideal woman. Will it have an effect on their feedback?(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Jacqui and Jane from the Oak Tree Cottage want to find out why Norman and Lorraine wouldn't return, while Martin and Catherine from the Busfeild Arms have some explaining to do when it comes to their profit margins. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Cameras follow Chester Zoo's four new fledgling Humboldt penguins who are being brought up apart from the main colony until they're old enough to compete for fish in the main pool. They're keen to make a break for it and join the adults, but when they're finally free to go they have other ideas. Elsewhere, Niall the chimpanzee keeper has known chimp Boris for 44 years. When the ape captures a moorhen chick, Niall must use his powers of persuasion to negotiate the safe release of his hostage(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer travel to the village of Clifford, West Yorkshire, to meet Vic and Paul Miller, who are unsure whether to sell or upgrade their house. Vic feels that the pair should look for a new home more suitable for them and their two young twins, but Paul believes they could use the budget to transform the property's layout and create a more functional family space(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer presents a celebrity edition of the words and numbers game, featuring contestants Jay Rayner and Jo Brand. With Susie Dent and Rachel Riley(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform(n)

programme.title: The Green Mile (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted child-killer of towering height and massive build is condemned to death row, but astonishes the guards with his good nature and miraculous healing powers. They believe the gentle giant is incapable of crime, and emotions run high as they realise there is no hope of overturning his death sentence. Supernatural drama, based on a novel by Stephen King, starring Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse and Harry Dean Stanton(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Neil, who is 53, is rushed into hospital with severe chest and lung injuries after coming off his bike, and 40-year-old Michael is brought in with chest pain and a heart rate of up to 180bpm after collapsing on a golf course. Lastly, doctors treat 27-year-old George, who accidentally grazed his eye on a low hanging branch(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Brooklyn, New York, to tackle a 55-year-old family business. Owner John Esposito left school at the age of 14 and has worked at his parents' restaurant, Sal's Pizzeria, ever since. Their success meant they could afford to purchase the location next door in the 1990s, naming it Mama Maria after John's mother, who died in 2002. But when he lost his father as well, John became overwhelmed by the responsibility of running both establishments and his livelihood is at risk(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp learns how to create a flower arrangement for the petite category at the Royal Cornwall Show, under the instruction of master florist Sioned Rowlands(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Julie Okine and Simon Sheriff, a pair of barristers in London, aim to fulfil a long-held dream of buying a holiday home in Spain, after Julie inherited some money from her mother. With a cash budget of £100,000 in mind, Laura Hamilton shows them five very different properties along the eastern Costa del Sol(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Laura and Leah Wright from Surrey have been enjoying holidays together in Spain since Leah was a child. Laura has now decided to put the cash from a recent inheritance towards buying a holiday home on Spain's Orihuela Costa that they can enjoy throughout the year, so Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties for their budget of £65,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Justine Noades and Ashley Pollak have a serious love affair with Ibiza, having visited the island eight times together and got engaged and married there. Now that their nightclubbing days are over, they plan to start a family and are back on the island to look for their dream holiday getaway. Ben Hillman shows them five properties within their £270,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house-hunting around Brighton and Hove, where Phil helps young first-time buyers Charlie and Katy. Meanwhile, Kirstie is hunting for a new flat for Dani, who wants to put down fresh roots after renting for the past 12 months(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.title: Leaving Neverland: Michael Jackson and Me
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part documentary exploring the experiences of James Safechuck and Wade Robson, who were both befriended by Michael Jackson as young boys. They and their families were invited into his singular and wondrous world, entranced by the singer's fairy-tale existence as his career reached its peak. Through interviews with Safechuck and Robson, now in their 30s and 40s, as well as their mothers, wives and siblings, director Dan Reed's film presents a portrait of sustained abuse, and explores the complicated feelings that led both men to confront their experiences(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. programme.desc: Lucy attends a three-day trade conference to drum up business, but when she has little luck she resorts to flirting with the male delegates. Lee is outraged with her plan, however, and in an attempt to stop her he turns up at the event - posing as her husband. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee gets jealous when Lucy talks about her first love, so Daisy helps him turn the tables by setting him up with her gorgeous friend. However, he is surprised when things start moving very quickly between them. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Bobin (Secret Diary of a Call Girl), with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out

But Lee refuses to take it lying down and decides to fight for his flatmate's attention. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star, with a guest appearance by Simon Farnaby (Horrible Histories)(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from May 2018, with comedy writer Andy Hamilton and politician Sayeeda Warsi joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to France to explore the possibility that Jesus married Mary Magdalene, and they had children. He meets respected scholars, authors and historians to consider the theory the son of God's bloodline lives on today(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage(n)

programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict(n)

programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of three preserved steam railway lines - the South Devon Railway heritage line, the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway and the Great Central Railway. Sports scientist Megan Smith has a dream of tending the fire of a steam locomotive on the Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland Railway, and she stokes the bolier of NG143 and sets off along the 25 miles from Caernarfon to Porthmadog(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring ship graveyards, including Scapa Flow in Orkney, where German warships were scuttled after the First World War, and Mallows Bay, home to 200 wrecks colonised by wildlife(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Jerusalem in search of Solomon's Temple. When the Romans sacked the city in 70AD, the fabled Treasure of Solomons was believed to have been looted and taken back to Rome. However, it is possible it was hidden away by the High Priests. Archaeologists Dr Shimon Gibson and Kate Raphael have dedicated their lives working out what actually happened, and discuss whether it is possible that Solomonic and Roman treasures lie undiscovered under Jerusalem(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their rise in the 19th Century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering and transport, conquering dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Colin Salmon narrates a documentary exploring how the lives of humans and animals across a vast area of the planet are affected by the transforming power of the monsoon rains. The end of the dry season in northern Australia sees 300,000 flying foxes gathering to drink while running a gauntlet of freshwater crocodiles, while in Sumatra a female orang-utan guides her youngster to where she knows a fig tree fruits at this time of year. At the Delhi stock exchange, the price of rice escalates every day the rains fail to materialise and priests submerge themselves in water and beseech Varuna, god of water, to come(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The effect of the rainy season on wildlife in Asia, with floods forcing animals to flee from Kaziranga National Park in north-east India in search of the safety of the hills. In Cambodia, the Mekong river swells so much that water is forced back up one of its tributaries to fill the vast Tonle Sap lake - one of the most productive freshwater fisheries on Earth. Fresh grass draws nomads and their vast herds of livestock back to their homelands, but they are stalked by hungry wolves and hyenas, which move in at night for the kill. Colin Salmon narrates(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: The other side of the monsoon, as the winds bring dry air and drought. Budgerigars swarm in the Australian desert, gathering at a precious waterhole, and although birds of prey try to ambush them, they are mesmerised by the array of bright colours on show. On the island of Borneo, a bearded pig hunts for seafood on the beach, while in the Rajasthan desert, nomads haggle over the price of camels, which can provide vital transport during this lean season. The mugger crocodiles of Sri Lanka stalk the animals taking a much-needed drink and a young leopard learning to hunt discovers there is no such thing as an easy meal. Colin Salmon narrates(n)

Cynthia, Sam and their daughters find their chicken guests highly entertaining, while Dawn, John, Max and Sam look after two piglets - and as they learn more about pig intelligence they research more about the health implications of processed meat, forcing them to question their entire attitude to what they eat(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan convinces Charlie that he needs to date more respectable women and sets him up with a judge called Linda, but the bachelor shows his inexperience in dating woman of his own age. programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is forced to come up with different ways to keep his girlfriend's four-year-old son amused. Meanwhile Alan becomes a test subject for a drug study to raise some funds. Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer star(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Lettings agent John wants to turn a former Blackpool hotel into bedsits, but a routine check on the property reveals not only a severely neglected house, but also a drugs den littered with paraphernalia and some of the worst conditions he has ever witnessed(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor set out to evict a tenant from a property in Manchester but when the man claims he knew nothing about the court order tempers begin to fray. A car salesman in Cumbria is not happy about paying £4,500 to a dissatisfied customer, and in Shropshire a pub licensee claims she is unable to pay the rent she owes to her ex-landlord. With emotions running high, can she cut a new deal?(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Stewart is joined by Vic in Liverpool to track down a landlord who owes his former tenants deposits totalling £5,000, while Gareth and Mitch are in for a surprise when they chase a builder in Wiltshire(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Chloe confronts Pierce over his statement about her job. Kyle is unsure of his ability to take Toadie up on his offer, but begins to gain confidence after they try a few tests(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mackenzie gives Colby time to sort out Bella's issues, but confirms she will not wait forever. Jasmine and Robbo return with Grace, only for Justin to demand custody(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A legendary jazz musician who believes he has a fatal brain disease is brought into the hospital with pneumonia and signs a form to ensure he is not resuscitated. However, House insists that the diagnosis is incorrect and administers treatment without consent - leading to a furious confrontation with the authorities. Guest starring R'n'B singer Brandy(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A homeless woman's worsening symptoms baffle Dr House and the team, who struggle to identify her condition - a task made more difficult by the fact she is unable to tell them her name. They finally make a diagnosis, but quickly need to find a mystery man known only as James before the patient dies. Medical drama, starring Hugh Laurie, with Robert Sean Leonard and Jesse Spencer(n)

Plus, a newlywed bride who was late for her reception when her elevator stopped working just feet away from her destination(n)

Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A student counsellor is murdered in New York, and Benson and Stabler investigate a possible connection to her volunteer work at a refugee centre. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A college student is murdered in an attack bearing all the hallmarks of a notorious serial killer. programme.title: Doctors In The War Zone
programme.desc: The United Nations estimates almost 200,000 people have been killed since Syria's civil war began, with many hundreds of thousands more injured. Millions have been forced from their homes, but the full human cost of the conflict is little known to the outside world because of the danger Western journalists face in trying to enter areas not controlled by the government. Most doctors have fled from those locations and many hospitals have been destroyed, but this documentary follows British trauma surgeon David Nott as he makes the perilous journey across the border into Syria to work alongside medics who have stayed behind. He carries out life-saving surgery in a facility forced underground by daily bombardment(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A woman suffering from psoriasis and a hip condition plans to retire with her husband to the healthier climes of the Dominican Republic. Zilpah Hartley shows them around a villa with a pool, a beachside house and a tropical garden property(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of 'bionic' vet Noel Fitzpatrick, who uses pioneering surgical techniques at his state-of-the-art centre in Surrey to save the lives of critically ill pets from around the country. In the first episode he tries to replace a cat's two paws after they were severed by a combine harvester, and tackles a Labrador's crippling arthritis(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman challenge four teams of stars from the world of music to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Former Marillion lead singer Fish teams up with Scottish folk star Barbara Dickson, while Haircut 100's Nick Heyward joins forces with pop rocker Toyah Willcox. Heather Small, formerly the lead singer of M People, joins the Neasden Queen of Soul Mari Wilson, and Liberty X's Jessica Taylor partners her former bandmate Kelli Young. As ever, the contestants strive to correctly guess the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings(n)
